---
title: "4.13 Implementing 2D Array Algorithms"
subtitle: "Data Collections"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit4-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## Topic Intro

### 2D algorithms combine nested loops with array patterns

Common 2D array algorithms include:

::: {.tight-list}
- sum and average
- min and max
- search a subsection
- detect duplicates
- rotate or reverse rows and columns
:::

## Sum

### Accumulate every cell

```java
int sum = 0;
for (int row = 0; row < grid.length; row++)
{
    for (int col = 0; col < grid[row].length; col++)
    {
        sum += grid[row][col];
    }
}
```

The nested loop visits every element exactly once.

## Average

### Count cells as well as total

```java
int sum = 0;
int count = 0;
for (int[] row : grid)
{
    for (int value : row)
    {
        sum += value;
        count++;
    }
}
double average = (double) sum / count;
```

Counting works even if rows have different lengths.

## Max

### Initialize from an existing cell

```java
int max = grid[0][0];
for (int row = 0; row < grid.length; row++)
{
    for (int col = 0; col < grid[row].length; col++)
    {
        if (grid[row][col] > max)
        {
            max = grid[row][col];
        }
    }
}
```

## Search a Subsection

### Bounds can restrict rows or columns

```java
for (int row = 0; row < 3; row++)
{
    for (int col = 0; col < grid[row].length; col++)
    {
        if (grid[row][col] == target)
        {
            return true;
        }
    }
}
return false;
```

Only the first three rows are searched.

## Duplicates

### Use a seen structure or compare cells

For small classroom examples, use a helper list of seen values:

```java
ArrayList<Integer> seen = new ArrayList<Integer>();
for (int[] row : grid)
{
    for (int value : row)
    {
        if (seen.contains(value))
        {
            return true;
        }
        seen.add(value);
    }
}
return false;
```

## Rotate and Reverse

### Trace positions before coding

For a single row:

```java
int left = 0;
int right = row.length - 1;
while (left < right)
{
    int temp = row[left];
    row[left] = row[right];
    row[right] = temp;
    left++;
    right--;
}
```

Start with one row before generalizing to the whole 2D array.

## Classroom Check

### A strong answer should...

::: {.tight-list}
- use nested loops for 2D sum, average, min, and max
- initialize min or max from an existing cell
- count cells when computing an average
- restrict traversal bounds for subsection searches
- trace index movement before rotating or reversing data
:::

## End

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)
